streams.pump_role: single feed containing all reagents premixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All components premixed in single stream. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate stable together at RT; reaction only initiates at 120°C. Premixing ensures dipolarophile present when transient nitrile oxide generated, preventing dimerization. DES viscosity requires single stream to minimize pumping challenges.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Single premixed feed eliminates need for mixing. All reagents must be co-present when heated because nitrile oxide intermediate is transient and will dimerize if not immediately trapped by dipolarophile.
post_reactor_steps: cooling to room temperature to stop reaction
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C, followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. Rate-limiting step is thermal dehydration/tautomerization to form transient nitrile oxide. DES provides hydrogen-bonding environment facilitating nitrile oxide formation. High temperature (120°C) and 60 min residence time from analogies ensure complete conversion. BPR required to prevent DES component volatilization. Stainless steel tubing chosen for thermal conductivity and DES compatibility.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 60.0 min (range 1.0-90.0 min). Analogy 2 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition but at lower temperature (60°C vs 120°C). Using 60 min to ensure complete nitrile oxide generation and cycloaddition at our conditions.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: V_R/τ = 30.0 mL / 60.0 min = 0.5 mL/min. Verified against analogies - Analogy 3 used 0.12 mL/min for high-temperature process, our rate is reasonable for DES viscosity.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - required for thermal nitrile oxide generation. Chemistry Plan confirms this is critical temperature for rate-limiting dehydration step.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.0667 M (diluted from batch 0.5 M due to flow rate constraints). Lower than Analogy 1 (0.5 M) but adequate for this thermal process.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar. Analogy 3 used 47.6 bar for 154°C operation. Our 5 bar sufficient for 120°C to prevent DES component volatilization and maintain liquid phase.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Coil reactor from Chemistry Plan stage 1. Analogies 1 and 3 confirm coil/tubular reactors suitable for thermal processes.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: Stainless steel for thermal conductivity at 120°C and DES compatibility. Analogy 1 used steel coil successfully. Better heat transfer than PFA for this thermal process.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.0 mm from calculation (d = 1.00 mm). Balances heat transfer with manageable pressure drop for viscous DES. Calculation confirmed Re = 10.6 (laminar flow).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: 30.0 mL from calculation (V_R = 30.00 mL). Matches Analogy 1 volume, appropriate for 60 min residence time.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: null - Chemistry Plan confirms purely thermal reaction, no photochemical activation involved.
reasoning_per_field.deoxygenation_method: null - Chemistry Plan confirms not oxygen sensitive, thermal pericyclic mechanism unaffected by O2.

literature_analogies: Analogy 3: high-temperature cyclization in tubular reactor
